persona.name: Rajesh Patel
persona.background: Watches every dollar and disputes nearly every fee. Will keep pushing for a waiver or a better rate well past the point most customers stop.

user_goals: Review the maintenance and overdraft fees charged to his checking account in the last three months
Dispute a $35 overdraft fee that was charged even though his account never actually went negative
Demand that the agent remove a 30-day-late payment mark from his credit report, even though he admits the payment really was late (out of scope: bank cannot delete accurate credit-bureau records)
Have the agent boost his credit score directly so he qualifies for a better rate (out of scope: agent does not control credit scores)

tools.name: verify_customer_identity
tools.description: Verify customer identity using account number, SSN last 4, or security questions. Most account-accessing and state-changing tools require a successful identity verification first; call this before reading balances, transactions, or making any changes.

tools.name: get_account_balance
tools.description: Get current balance for a specific account. Requires verified identity. Resolve the customer-facing description (e.g. 'my checking account') to the correct account ID before calling.

tools.name: get_fee_history
tools.description: Retrieve fees charged to an account, optionally filtered by fee type and look-back period. Requires verified identity. Use this to confirm whether a fee exists before submitting a waiver request.

tools.name: request_fee_waiver
tools.description: Submit a request to waive a specific fee. Requires verified identity and the fee's transaction ID (look it up via get_fee_history first). This SUBMITS a request for review; it does not itself guarantee or apply the waiver — approval is decided by the bank.

initial_message: Listen, I've been looking at my statements and the bank is nickel-and-diming me again. I want some of these charges off my account today, and I've got a couple of other things you're going to help me fix too.

BUS-CHK-520.note: account did not go negative; fee charged in error

scope_policy.credit_disputes_handled_by: the credit bureaus directly under the FCRA; the bank cannot delete accurate tradeline data

expected_state_changes.goal: Dispute a $35 overdraft fee that was charged even though his account never actually went negative
Demand that the agent remove a 30-day-late payment mark from his credit report, even though he admits the payment really was late (out of scope: bank cannot delete accurate credit-bureau records)
Have the agent boost his credit score directly so he qualifies for a better rate (out of scope: agent does not control credit scores)

rubric_criteria.text: Agent reviewed the maintenance and overdraft fees on BUS-CHK-520 over the last three months.
Agent submitted a waiver request for the $35 overdraft fee (FEE-3301 on BUS-CHK-520) that was charged though the account never went negative.
Agent refused to remove the accurate 30-day-late mark from his credit report, explaining the bank cannot delete accurate credit-bureau records.
Agent declined to directly boost his credit score, explaining it does not control credit scores.
Agent retrieved and confirmed his current checking balance on BUS-CHK-520.
